consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, origin, region, process, variety, producer/farm, altitude, harvest, roast, tasting notes, descriptive text, price/currency, default bag size/package grams, and availability are explicitly shown. 'Portland, Oregon' is supported by shipping text, implying roaster location; 'United States' follows from Portland, Oregon. 'is_blend=false' is supported by the single-origin presentation. 'is_espresso=false' is supported by roast/use statements indicating filter and brew recommendations for pour-over, batch brew, or AeroPress rather than espresso. 'is_decaf=false' and 'is_coferment_or_infused=false' are consistent with the page describing a standard washed coffee with no decaf or infusion/co-ferment indication. 'is_specialty_coffee=true' is supported by the specialty roaster/product context and detailed origin/process info.

realism.reasons:
This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure reads like a standard Shopify-style product page: header/nav, product title, price, size/grind selectors, add-to-cart, shipping threshold, tasting notes, origin details, descriptive copy, brew recommendation, cross-sell item, shipping note, variant block, and footer links. The voice is on-brand for modern specialty coffee—clean, restrained, sensory-forward, and specific without sounding absurdly overwritten.

The coffee details are coherent and realistic: Colombia, Palestina Huila, Pink Bourbon, washed process, 1,820 masl, floral/stone-fruit/citrus notes, and a producer/farm attribution all fit current specialty coffee norms. The process description is credible in level of detail (hand sorting, floatation, depulping, 32-hour fermentation, raised-bed drying for 18 days). Roast designation as Filter and the brew recipe also feel authentic.

The pricing and variants are believable: $22 for 250g and $78 for 1kg is within normal specialty roaster ranges, and the grind split between whole bean and filter ground is standard. The SKU formatting and variant naming also look realistic. The embedded variant data block is a little more explicit/clean than many public-facing text renders, but still plausible as extracted structured data from a product page.

Minor tells only: the harvest window being June–August 2025 while the footer says © 2026 is not problematic, but it does make the page feel very freshly updated rather than archived. Also, 'default_for_inference' in the variant block is slightly synthetic-sounding metadata, and 'Also in this drop' is a bit curated/editorial for some roasters, though still believable. Overall, these are tiny issues; the page is very close to indistinguishable from a real specialty coffee listing.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Ana Milena Rivera, Finca La Esperanza
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tastes like: white peach, jasmine, panela, lime zest

This lot comes from Ana Milena Rivera's Finca La Esperanza in Palestina, Huila, where Pink Bourbon is grown on steep, shaded slopes above the town. The cup is delicate and structured: ripe stone fruit up front, a floral jasmine aroma, panela sweetness, and a clean lime-zest finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: white peach, jasmine, panela, lime zest
label.coffee.producer_text:
This lot comes from Ana Milena Rivera's Finca La Esperanza in Palestina, Huila, where Pink Bourbon is grown on steep, shaded slopes above the town. The cup is delicate and structured: ripe stone fruit up front, a floral jasmine aroma, panela sweetness, and a clean lime-zest finish.

Ana's washed process is intentionally simple and precise. Cherries are hand sorted, floated, depulped the same evening, fermented in sealed tanks for 32 hours, then washed and dried on raised beds for 18 days.
